prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the BigQuery API enabled. Note its project ID, which you supply in the connector's project_id field.

A service account in that project with permission to read and query BigQuery data. Grant it the BigQuery Data Viewer role (roles/bigquery.dataViewer) for the data and the BigQuery Job User role (roles/bigquery.jobUser) to run query jobs.
A service account key downloaded in JSON format, which you paste into the credentials_json field. The key file can only be downloaded once, so store it securely.
Optionally, the ID of the BigQuery dataset to read from if you want to scope the connector to a single dataset via the dataset_id field.

authMethods.name: Service account JSON key (credentials_json)
authMethods.summary: BigQuery authenticates with a Google Cloud service account. Create a service account in your project, grant it roles/bigquery.dataViewer and roles/bigquery.jobUser, then create a JSON key for it. Provide the full contents of the downloaded JSON key file in the credentials_json secret field. The key includes the service account email, private key, and project ID.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Google Cloud project
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud console, select (or create) the project that contains your BigQuery data and enable the BigQuery API for it. Copy the project ID; you will enter it in the connector's project_id field, which is required.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account with BigQuery access
setupSteps.body: Create a service account in the same project. Grant it the BigQuery Data Viewer role (roles/bigquery.dataViewer) so it can read and export table data, and the BigQuery Job User role (roles/bigquery.jobUser) so it can run query jobs. Job User can only be granted at the project, folder, or organization level.

setupSteps.title: Generate and download a JSON key
setupSteps.body: Open the service account, go to the Keys tab, click Add key, then Create new key, and choose JSON. The console downloads a service account key file in JSON format. This file can only be downloaded once, so save it securely.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the full contents of the downloaded JSON key file into the credentials_json field. Enter your project ID in project_id. Optionally set dataset_id to limit the connector to a specific BigQuery dataset; leave it blank to use the project default scope.

sources.title: Create and delete service account keys
